reviews.review_text:
I remember when Metro 2033 came out I saw a dude playing it and immediately thought it was literally Russian knockoff Fallout.

I was so young and stupid.
Metro Exodus is yet another fantastic addition to the post apocalyptic genre

I would never imagine playing the Metro saga, but I gave it an opportunity and I love it. This game, comparing to the other Metro, gives you much more hours of gameplay and so many exploring and open world maps. The graphics are amazing, how can it be like six or seven years old and looking better than more modern games?. Damn, I love this game and I am enjoying the story so much.

[h1]A larger but emptier world.[/h1]

The metro games have had quite an overhaul since Last Light. The gameplay has been changed from a previously linear story to one where you're dropped in a large, open area which you get to explore freely.
Initially there's nothing wrong with that. But having played the original games [i](As in not the REDUX, but ACTUAL first 2033 and first last light game)[/i] there's some changes made that i understand but that also ruins the experience. 
It falls into the pitfall of so many games where people just screamed that they wanted an open world to explore, demanding width, but never caring about depth. 
As a result you get a large world that you can explore but got very little reason to explore. 

[u][b]Pre-war bullets as currency for trading is gone.[/b][/u]
Long gone are the traders where before you could sell lesser ammo which you could carry a limited number of in order to get pre-war bullets that you could carry an infinite number of. 40 Pistol bullets, 90 Rifle bullets, 24 Shotgun shells and 50 Ball bearing balls for the pneumatic guns is what you got for a good while. 

[u][b]Lootable ammo is virtually gone[/b][/u]
Instead a lot of focus is put on the crafting bench, where you can craft every ammo type so long as you have enough of only 2 resources that are used for literally everything:
*Chemicals 
*And scrap
There's no specialized parts that are rarer, you loot a corpse, you get scrap and chemicals, you loot a locker, it has scrap in it. Pick up some weird fungus or mutated fauna, just generic chemical resource.
I remember in the old metro games that you found ammo on corpses and what you could see is what you picked up, if you didn't look around the side of the corpse then maybe you'd miss a magazine on his belt, or a strip of shotgun shells on him. Of course, this meant that there was a developer that was tasked with putting all this ammo on the corpses which, if you make the game open world would likely take ages. But it added to the experience of the game.
Now its the same two generic corpses. The laying down one that you flip up on its side, and the sitting down that as you loot it they fall to the side. No ammo or anything visible on them, they are just a loot chest for you to click on and recieve generic resources.
And you can use these  to craft whichever ammo you need, compared to the old game where you might have to switch your playstyle and weapon depending on what ammo you find until you come across a trader to stock up. 
So you might have had a good shotgun, but weren't finding any shells and were running low on assault rifle ammo, so you had to drop your shotgun to pick up a Pistol instead because you have plenty of that ammo. Here there's no need. Pick up a few cans, a few mushrooms and boom, you can make assault rifle bullets. 

[u][b]Trading is gone[/b][/u]
With the new crafting system you can go to a workbench which are spread out across locations in the different places you visit. There you can attach any accessory to your gun assuming you've looted it before. 
At the train which becomes your hub you can swap out your weapon for any weapon you've picked up on your journey. So there's no incentive anymore to pick up a weapon you find out and about and try to bring it in order to sell it because its expensive and worth selling, or because you want to strip it of its parts but that kind of crafting was only available in stations before. But not anymore because you can just do that right as you find the weapon. There's no incentive to carry anything with you and make back and fourth trips.

I understand that people now days find such things tedious but the thing is the old developers didn't make the games that way because of limitations or something. They made the games that way to force you to experience the game this way. You're either a loot goblin and stock up like crazy beforehand if you don't want to end up needing to drop your precious 400 bullet expensive weapon, or you had to pick up and use any weapon you found. And unless you were at a station with an armory, if all you had was a single barrel shotgun, then that's what you had to make do with. 

This was Metro's first jump into open world so i'm sure the developers either didn't have time for it or felt like it was too big of a jump from the previous two games to throw a too complex crafting system at the player. We'll see what the upcomming Metro 2039 is like, if it expands on and makes the open world more lived in and gives you a reason to explore it or if its as shallow and boring as Exodus and pretty much every open world Ubisoft game.

For me though, Exodus was a letdown compared to the previous two games. I'd rather take linear and lived in rather than wide open and shallow.

Metro Exodus is a game born from the passion and love for the series from both the wonderful members, past and present, of 4A Games, and Dmitry Glukhovsky, filled with the love and emotions of everyone involved. And you can feel all of that every step of the journey.

A perfect end for Artyom's saga, the game reaches amazing heights through every bit of non-required, but very much needed extra details. The opening animation of the backpack, the bounces of the weapons while customizing, and the personal conversations of the characters. A heart string pulling, human story of hope and to keep moving forward. 

The music especially, is one of the biggest emotional highlights throughout the game. It adds on to every moment it is present, it brings tension, release, triumph, defeat. Every note a masterpiece, and every track a memorable moment.

[h1] <><><><><><><><><><><><><><> [/h1]

With the invasion and occupation of Crimea in 2014, and now with the invasion and war of Ukraine since 2022, everyone involved with the Metro series have been personally affected by the conflict. Dmitry Glukhovsky has openly opposed the invasion, he has been forced out of his home and is wanted in Russia for these views. 4A Games as well, have had some of its members killed while defending their home. 

Now is more important than ever to support not just the people involved with Metro, but Ukraine as a whole in defending itself from the horrific and ongoing pillaging and destructive Russian invaders. Support 4A Games and Dmitry Glukhovsky, support Ukrainian fundraisers, and support the Ukrainian servicemen and volunteers in anyway that you are able to. 

[h2] Русский военный корабль, иди на ♥♥♥ [/h2]

This is going to break my heart to say..

I absolutely LOVED the Metro series (the game, I have yet to read the books) until I've witnessed Deep Silver's blatant disrespectful treatment of their fanbase. A person died during the development of this game and you have the disgusting nerve to pin a statement about how we need to send you our PC info so you can "fix" a DECADE old issue in-game? People are having to restart entire Ironman playthroughs cause you don't know what's wrong with the game YOU MADE? It's YOUR JOB to make the game optimal for as many PC's builds as possible. When did game development all of a sudden become a player's concern? WE ALREADY PAID YOU FOR THE ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ GAME! If you can't handle the workload, seek a new avenue of employment respectfully. Don't piss on your player base and the memory of the person who passed whilst developing this game with your pseudo-intellectual nonsense. I've played Metro since Last Light on the Xbox 360 and even that didn't have the amount of glitches and bugs that the "PC Enhanced" or even the PC version in general has. I should've seen the writing on the walls then when you tried removing Ranger Hardcore mode from the game and tried to sell it back to us only if we pre-ordered the game. It took Angry Joe lighting a fire under your asses for you to put it back in the game for free. I do still play the game from time to time because I will give credit where credit is due and one thing I will say is the foundation (weapons, characters, and environment) are totally fine but the game-breaking bugs like corrupted saves, constant crashing, audio clipping, stealth gameplay bugs (bandits already being aggroed before you even show up to the place), and the shady developers are too much for me to truly recommend this game to anyone. If you're reading this and you took offense Deep Silver, prove me wrong. 

Before anyone tries to 'check' me on my Metro journey. I bought this game on PS5 and The Series S Xbox. I beat both of them and they have (mostly) the same bugs like constant crashing.

Metro Exodus is one of the most atmospheric shooters ever made.
Few games manage to create the same feeling of isolation, tension, and immersion while exploring a destroyed world. The combination of survival mechanics, scarce resources, brutal firefights, and incredible environmental design makes the journey across post-apocalyptic Russia unforgettable.

What makes the game special is how immersive everything feels. You manually wipe your gas mask, charge your flashlight, clean your weapons, and scavenge every bullet like your life depends on it - because sometimes it actually does. The game constantly makes you feel vulnerable, especially during nighttime exploration or while traveling through dark underground sections.

The shift from the claustrophobic metro tunnels to larger open areas works surprisingly well and gives the world much more variety while still keeping the signature Metro atmosphere intact. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

The good:
- Incredible atmosphere and immersion
- One of the best post-apocalyptic worlds in gaming
- Excellent environmental storytelling
- Resource management adds real tension
- Beautiful visuals and lighting
- Weapon customization feels great
- Strong survival-horror elements
- Exploration is genuinely rewarding
- Sound design is phenomenal
- The train crew and character interactions make the journey memorable

The bad:
- Animations and movement can feel clunky
- Some voice acting and dialogue feel awkward
- AI can behave strangely at times
- Open-world sections occasionally lose pacing
- Bugs and technical issues still happen
- Stealth mechanics can feel inconsistent
- Certain areas drag on longer than needed
- Moral system can feel restrictive if you want the best ending
- Loading times can be annoying on older hardware

Overall, Metro Exodus is one of those rare games where the atmosphere alone carries huge parts of the experience. It’s rough around the edges sometimes, but the immersion, world design, and constant tension make it one of the best single-player shooters in years.
I Really Enjoyed this series, i will try to complete all 100% achievement, Recommended  10/10

a 10/10 game with full DLC especially with Russian dub. beautiful experience and vibe and notices every detail and exploring along the way its amazing. looking forward to play it more
would def recommend it <3

The semi open-world works so well in the Metro universe. The new weapons and equipment upgrade system is a nice improvement from the previous games. The only thing I missed is more underground Metro time, but that's probably due to the lore. 
Most complete Metro game... until now
got stuck in sewers and got attacked by beasts then saved by my friends, went home and played metro exodus, 10/10 would reccomend this game
10/10 game , it is my favorite type of games, survival, stealth, shooting and mutants

The first half of the game is very cool but oh my god after the desert the story becomes unbearable and the performance gets worse (no raytracing enabled).  Seriously the dialog is mediocre and the human enemies are unbelievably boring.  World is cool though.  

Other than that I have nothing to say, gameplay is good, very immersive, the human characters spoil it for me.

ufak tefek bugları az buçuk can sıksa da gayet keyifli bir oyun.
uzun süre oynadıktan sonra ekran bi anda donup kalıyo, kapatıp açmaktan başka bir şey yapılmıyo bu sorunu bir tek ben mi yaşadım bilmiyorum.

[h1] 6/10 [/h1]
Man I was so excited for this game after palying both 2033 and last light and finishing them last month. This ♥♥♥♥ is ASS, the story is ASS(the beggining is so slow), the new crafting system is ASS. Good graphics tho. Most of the time I had to punch my enemies instead of firing at them. This game should have remained mainly a shooter instead of all this unnecessary open world design. I guess my issue coming to this game was that I came to this right after "last light", if there were a couple years between my playthroughs I would have enjoyed it more maybe.

This is not Metro. I know a lot of people love this game, maybe because of the graphics or something else. But for me this is not what metro is about. When I hear Metro it instantly brings me back to that hardcore underground life where there is an actual story. But in this game, the story begins above ground and continues for a while and I don't like that about this game.

I'm glad I finally got around to playing Exodus with the next game in the series announced recently. As an Epic exclusive title (for a year) this was a crappy taste and I was put off playing it. I eventually bought it heavily discounted on Steam. Take note, 4A, no Epic exclusive this time around, please. 

My favourite level was the open, vast, dry and bright expanse of the Caspian, which nicely contrasted the confined tunnels of Metro. Its cues and homages were clear; open-world games like MGS:V and Far Cry (e.g. in-game map and vehicle use). This sense of space and freedom was a breath of fresh air for the series, and all the best moments of the game built upon this theme. 

My main criticism would be the laborious cut scenes that can't be skipped. It also has heaps of unnecessary animations and jank, particularly in boats and around water that can often get you killed. The script and voice acting are brie levels of cheese (I wish there was a native language with subtitles option. "Colonel Mee-ler"? Really?). It's also extremely resource-hungry even with a PC built half a decade after it was released, and will test your rig at 4K - the Enhanced Edition even more so (it requires a GPU capable of ray-tracing). Turning on the flashlight also universally halved my frame rates. The game just really hated me having my own flashlight source. This is a major defect as far as I'm concerned because the game can be so dark and turning on the light is an essential feature, so I was stuck with less than satisfactory performance for many parts of the game. Fortunately you acquire night vision goggles that thankfully flatten the lighting and boost frame rates. 

Technical issues aside, the gunplay and stealth is excellent; the weapon modification system is versatile; the world is fully immersive with a 24-hour light cycle; and the art is top-tier with excellent models and convincing scenery. 

I remember Exodus received a lot of accolades at the time, I suppose 2019 was a fallow year for AAA releases and I'm not convinced it deserved such high praise. It's still a very good game and worth checking out before the next instalment appears.
Finally finished this game after owning it for years. Highly recommend. Make sure you play on Hardcore.

Exodus is Magnum Opus of the Metro series without a doubt

Despite many changes and tweaks it is still the Metro series that i fell in love with, it's the same but adorned with new and fancy drip

Locations are beautiful and fun to explore, gameplay despite various changes it still has the soul of the previous games 

Story and it's ending, was great, one of the best ones i had pleasure experiencing and the (good) ending gives Artyom the conclusion of his story that he did deserve

To conclude it all, Metro:Exodus is very solid and well optimized must-play, it goes obviously for the whole trilogy but this game on it's own is definitely one of the games of all time

Metros, one of my favorite franchise. I really love post-apocalyptic games and see how creative the devs are with the map design, enemy's, weapons and story. The world looks beautifully ugly and thats my favorite part and get rewarded for being curious and explore the world.

The game is fantastic, highly recommended!!! The story is captivating and keeps you engaged from start to finish. The Sam’s Story expansion feels like a full game on its own, and I really enjoyed it. Looking forward to the next installment of the Metro series

Metro Exodus is easily one of the best games I have ever played. Everything about it feels incredible, from the stunning graphics and immersive world to the amazing gameplay and satisfying combat. Exploring the different regions was always exciting, and the attention to detail is simply outstanding. What truly makes the game special, however, is its story and characters. The journey with Artyom and the Aurora crew felt emotional, memorable, and full of unforgettable moments. A true masterpiece that kept me hooked from beginning to end.

As for Metro 2033 and Metro: Last Light, they were also fantastic experiences. Both games built an amazing atmosphere, delivered great stories, and introduced memorable characters that made the entire Metro trilogy one of the most unique and enjoyable gaming experiences I've had.

A massive upgrade over the previous Metro games. The series success really shows here. I love the half open world, I'm 10 hours in and still exploring the first chapter. For the price of a MC burger menu you can buy entire franchise when it's on sale :D
this game is so much different from previous 2 games and its beautiful ill forever treasure this game  def worth for its price

OPEN WORLD DONE RIGHT! this game came out when making various franchises "open world" was a fad. But Metro Exodus did it right. Still retained the cinematic feeling but also made the player feel like their choices had weight. 10/10

I mean... the achievements are annoying to get but the combat is great. Other than some bugs (especially on the desert level) the game is great. It is sad to see the DLCs block my grind to 100% tho, I'm far away anyway (41% completion)

Works Well on my laptop at 720p, atleast 30 fps at low Settings.

I have a 2 fan cooler a generic one you will find in amazon or in my case Shopee or Lazada, combined with Performance Mode Fan Settings, Plugged in, and playing at night time due to my country's horrendous heat index (Still at 12pm with no air conditioning it runs well although my laptop cant handle the heat and I don't recommend it at all to play it at peak heat hours.) 

Story is so superb! Getting the good ending is not as hard as it was before in the last 2 games. (I suggest reading the guides to get it). 

Gameplay encourages exploring to get the materials, upgrades, notes, postcards and secrets laying around. (I only played the hard mode -not the hardest- just to test my skills in my thinking and survival skills and so far I'm doing well.)

Graphics although I play at low settings is still great and while hoping to get a new computer soon for the new Metro game it still sits in my 10/10 optimized yet beautiful game list. In High Settings the details are amazing and spot on. (Even if I only have like 2 - 6 fps while playing it due to my laptop specs.)

Weirdly charming. Not necessarily the most polished game, in terms of mechanics. Gameplay works well enough. However, the story, characters, and presentation are true genre highlights, creating an unmatchable atmosphere.

looked like a game that i would love, but it really wasn't. i can get why people like it, it is really well made and has a good story, but it isnt very clear where you're supposed to go somewhere.

also, the enhanced edition that it comes with is super buggy. it says my specs can handle it just fine, but everytime i tried to start it, it would freeze and crash
Game has quality and it is still Metro. Mechanics and gunplay are good.

Well, First of all let me quickly establish my readiness for this game.

If you, like me, played trilogy without prior knowledge of the book material and in somewhat decent timeframe of, lets say a month, then we share more than enough.

Story wise, to my understanding, Metro 2033 is closely following the book, with some creative breathing from the developer team while second and third are original and do not align with source material. That is to say, first and second games are awesome as one might expect from story driven survival shooter. They are both great at telling the story and I ate them up in almost one sittings, partially cz they are not too long in playthrough, 2033 being around 7-9 hours and Last Light 11-13. 

The major change comes with third game where creators made decision of making game that is basically splits into several "arcs". A swamp, a dessert, lush forests and some episodical ones which I will not indulge for the sake of spoilers. In these "arcs" you get the freedom of movement unlike the first two games, one might even call it a semi-open world while still being story driven, it allows you to make some choices in the matter, rather than just lead you like Dark Souls trilogy would. And it is amazing, awesome design, beautiful setting of post apocaliptic Russia which touches multiple regions that most people actually forget that the country and its people are extremely different in their belief, language and traditions. Federation, though parody as it stands in current time, is a Federation of multiple ethnic groups with Slavic people making only majority, not the 100% of nations people.

I can rant about multiple topics on why the game is great, how much its worth the money or how amazing is this system of "arcs" they implemented in the third expansion but its already too long of a review so, yeah man, freaking buy it and play, shiets good.

Cyka Blyat 3: Exodus

Having played the previous installments, I'm happy to say I was satisfied with this entry. The devs have shaken some things up, however the core feel of Metro is still here.

I was pleased with the switch to a more open world map design, with all sorts of areas to explore, usually leading to better gear and side story content. I'm impressed with how much they have fleshed out this world. The NPCs, the enemies, even the mutants, it's all varied and keeps things interesting.

The looting system is still here and, not gonna lie, at times it does feel a little tedious. You'll run into a room and rather than taking in the vibes, you're just mashing E like a hungry loot goblin. However, it's still an engaging gameplay loop so I can't complain too much. Of course, you'd be scavenging like a madman in a post apocalyptic wasteland.

I was pleased with the story too. I won't drop any spoilers, just that I was satisfied with its conclusion and how it wraps in the DLCs as well. Honestly, I had a great time with this game and I think most people would too.

Especially if you've played the previous installments, or games in a similar setting like Fallout for example. Honestly, I kinda wish Fallout was more like this. Give it a go, it's fun!

Storywise, I feel it meets the expectations set by the previous entries. This post apocalyptic world is crafted to ever remind you of that fact, yet it does not want to you think there is no hope. The story is full of life, with a wonderful cast of characters. You can just run through the game quickly, sure, but if you do choose to soak up the very plentiful exposition and immerse in the atmosphere of it all, I'm confident you'll enjoy the time you put in.

Gameplay wise, I played on Hardcore Ranger with minimal UI. There is the more restrictive Full Dive mode, but I wouldn't recommend that until you finish the game at least once, but probably a couple times. That mode completely removes any UI, HUD, etc. You have to know where item pickups and interactables are, because you don't get an indicator for interaction.

Difficulty wise, I wouldn't say that Hardcore Ranger was particularly difficult. You will die very easy, but enemies die just as easily. I died a lot to wonky things, and I mean alot, like enemy pathing changing on reload or things otherwise not playing out in a way that it did prior. I did also die a fair bit of times due to my own stubborness. 

I think stealthing could have been a bit better and more accommodating, but as it was for me, it's overall fine. I had issues with being detected really easy sometimes, even though I shouldn't have because I didn't do anything to warrant detection. See above with regards to wonky deaths.

I would have liked to have more time with everyone in the crew, as well. Maybe not great in practice, but I would be interested in possible 'base building' in future installments.

Overall the game was well worth the price of admission, time and money wise. I look forward to sinking my teeth into the DLC and then later on Metro 2039.

Best of the trilogy, I think the variety in areas you visit and the likability of the crew are the games main strengths. The morality system is a little scuffed at times but still better than the first two games.

I recommend playing with Russian voice acting and English subtitles for the best experience.

I think the Metro series in general is successful and beautiful with every game. In this game, they stepped outside the first two games and tried something different; they went out into the outside world, which turned out nicely. But when I think of Metro, a dark, narrow, claustrophobic, and tense environment comes to mind, and I like that much more. I hope they prioritize this in the new game that will be released.

Spectacular finale. The opening scene sets up the final consequence of the game. The giant open world levels are a great enhancement. The final level in the dead city is the best metro experience I have had. Its so lonely and dead. The sound design, visual effects, and survival elements are all much elevated. 

The only things I did not enjoy was the saving feature was kind of wonky + the boat sections. Any section in a boat is terrible. 9/10
The atmosphere, the gameplay, the world building. Love it!!

Such a good Game where you feel the world. The Constant Dangers of Monsters, People, Radioactivity, running out of Ammo. Almost in every encounter you feel the Danger no Matter the good gear you have. Plus the Hidden Karma system, so cool loved it!
My only critic is sometimes it crahes, the reddot optic on guns can sometimes reflect the light in a manner where you cant see ♥♥♥♥.

Overall I would say this game is great story 10/10! The only complaint I have is the sluggish gameplay and the glitches with AI other which "fine" since you get used to it after playing for a bit. W game W everything worth the money!
nice graphics bastards, a good game about metro without the metro itself.
The game was good actually, we can explore by our self without any objective icon, and this my first gen

It was so fun and nostalgic playing the game that I was fond of when I was a kid. Now playing Metro Exodus, I can further appreciate how much effort and dedication have the developers poured into this game. And quite frankly, I was suprised to hear Tsoi on the Radio. Цой жив!

I loved playing this game I've beat it on console and my computer, its pretty re playable and it constantly goes on sale, I would suggest getting this game.

Didn't believe it when i first saw the reviews, but holy this is peak, people weren't lying when they said the immersion is so good, probably the best out of the games in the category.
An easy entry to pick up for me, as a  big fan of the Metro series this game takes the normal progression in a new and interesting light. solid 8/10.

♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ amzaing game, got to be one of my top 3 with the build up from the first 2 games everything hit s that much harder and feels so much better thats not including gun play gun sounds absolutely beuatiful the guns feel like gun heavy loud and powerful. visuals are something else they look gorgeous on my pc and i got a lemon not inculing this game has an enhanced edition with visual upgrades

gunplay 11/10

total; just play it thank me later

I just recently completed Metro Exodus story and it two dlcs, in my opinion this game is my least fav of the series, Don't get me wrong it is a beautiful game, I did like the new crafting mechanic they added into the game, the story was a little blend in the beginning but later on I'm became interesting and still felt the same love for the story as I did from the previous games. each level through the game I Found it awesome to explored when I can and even love levels we had to fight through. but some that was recommended for stealth was not as good as it was from the previous games I felt like but that just me. the moral system in this game is good too but it does get confusing of what do you do unless you read a guide how to earn or lose moral points. 

Most hating level was taiga. I did not enjoyed it at all and with the save system in this game its sucks, especially if I'm one of those people that manual save every 5, 10 mins, or even a hour and used multiple saves slots if I could. but with only 3 autosave slots and one quicksave slot. I feel like they should have added more quicksave slots or at least have two manual save slots with the quicksave, this is not as big of the deal cause I know I can do well in games but I wish they would have let us keep our progression stuff and equipment/materials that we the players have found throughout the story. but if we restarted the chapter/level we were on. it would only give us some things, zero materials, and not let us keep our weapon parts that we had found from previous levels. I wish they would allowed us keep our stuff like they did in the previous games if we needed to restart a chapter. but it is what it is. it was rough for me, and for some reason the camera felt really heavy to move, no matter how fast or slow you had the setting on and I even check the graphics settings it just still felt rough to move it. which is odd but before and after Taiga, the levels were very good. Each one was unique and had a different vibe towards it which I like, my two favorite levels were Yamanatau and The dead city I won't spoiled them incase someone reads this review.  

I enjoy the dlcs as well I can definitely say these were way better then the previous dlcs from the last games of the series. which was a nice plus for me. cause it can be rare as I see it to have good worth it dlcs to play more of the game you love or somewhat love in my opinion. 

I can say I would recommend to give this game a shot and complete the story and dlcs but if only you played previous metro games before this one. cause it is a sequel of Last Light and 2033. if you played this game first you will get Hella confused of where the story is going, once you beat it. After you done with it It does feel hard to get in the mood replay the main game story again that what I felt, but people can have different feels. once completed I cannot do it again, which is a shame cause I have play the previous games more times then I count from consoles to pc, and love them, nevertheless it is worth it to give a shot 

Here are the Pros and cons 
Pros
#1 The game world and level design is beautiful to see and played. As to the point I would use it to listing to lo-fi music or instrumental music with it.
#2 Story is a little blend from the beginning but it does get good later and I would say a little emotional but in a good way.
#3 Exploring and savaging loot from places and people makes it worth it. 
#4 the crafting system is good and gets better later in the game with the more stuff you collected from previous levels.
 #5 moral (karma) system is better in this game but a little confusing, it does make it worth it to do for the true ending of this game. 
#Bonus dlcs are worth it to play after you complete the main story of this game. First dlc "two colonels" then second dlc "Sam story" 

Cons 
#1 Save system is short with only 3 auto save slots and One quick save slot.
#2 Losing your equipment progressing and materials (if you needed too) by restarted chapters.  
#3 it does not feel worth it to play the main story again.
#4 moving the camera of your character feels very sluggage for some reason. 

That is all, but I hope this help you enjoy the game and story. 

Sincerely Statingbloom :)

A lot of people complain about this Metro game not taking place in the metro but honestly I love it more for that reason, the story had to develop in a unique way and the train and travelling to new and robust locations does that in an awesome way. I understand liking the more claustrophobic sense of horror in the metro, but saying that like this game doesn't also do claustrophobia well—sometimes better than the previous games—is silly.

This is the best game so far from the franchise, I like the open world concept, the graphic is fantastic, while not too many differences in gameplay, but overall this game is absolute cinema!

Without a doubt my favourite one out of the trilogy, love how both the DLCs build up from the base game's already amazing storyline. 
- The semi-open world aspect of this game is amazing, driving that rundown van in the Caspian desert was so much fun. 
- The visuals are breathtaking, especially on the level Taiga, the forest floor filled with foliage, dried leaves and fireflies filling up the night sky as you sneak your way in.
- Gun modifications along with the variety of ammo provided to you makes the selection of weapons you carry with you seem so personal.

One of the best things about these games is that exploration always rewards you, with a slight change in the story, a better relation with a certain character, some audio logs or notes that provide extra context, fun easter eggs or just plain old extra ammo and resources. 
It never goes to waste, signing off 🫡🚃🛤️
Perfect story, expect graphics. Waiting for Metro 2039.
Great game, story in the main game and dlcs was fantastic, even ran good on my potato pc.

The game is great with only two setbacks  (personally) 1st is that i feel like the game just doest reward you enough with your exploring. I mean sure you get all the upgrades just by searching but the ammo and stuff you need to survive isnt there some times, it feels heavy on the soul when u have to fight zombies, mutants or the bandits and waste 3/4 of your invetory just to recieve few crafting items
amazing game. good graphics. nice story. fun to play.
I find the games story written extremely well and the gameplay good for a first person shooter I fell in love with the characters and I am sure others would too. I highly recommend.

Great game, story telling is the thing! Also game engine and animations are state of the art for 2019 and even now!
Different style from the other Metro games but still works. The open level system is fun and well done overall. Memorable characters and the atmosphere is great as always.

I first played this game on a 1080 Ti, right before the 2020 upgrade craze and the 3080 came out.

I had just finished Metro Last Light Redux (unforgettable experience) and this game was an advertisement for RTX which I finally got to experience on the 3080. It blew my mind. I played it til about halfway through until I realized there was an enhanced version which I installed and somehow lost my progress, and felt demotivated to continue playing.

Fast forward to 2026 in a broken gaming world full of early access slop, failed launches and downgraded graphics, I reinstalled Metro Exodus Enhanced Edition.

To my surprise, I had forgotten a lot of the Exodus story, and I am now playing it on a 5070 TI on a 240hz OLED gaming monitor and… wow.

This game is incredibly immersive, it’s practically an interactive 3D novel. There is such rich detail, and the dialogue is incredible.

I don’t typically like “end of the world” type games, but there is something so cozy, and so warm about this game and the story it’s telling.

It slows you down, has you thinking about the little things in life that we take for granted.

This is a must-play. If you own this game and stumbled onto this review, replay it with new hardware or just new eyes. We lost something today, that this game had a few years ago. This is peak gaming.
The game is very fun once it grows on you, I recommend not turning on RTX and DLSS. It doesn't feel a lot like Metro at the start, but it sure as hell is.

Exodus is incredibly ambitious and a technically challenging project in comparison to the previous installments, and it's damn impressive how much this Ukrainian powerhouse continues to grow and woo us from one game to the next. Exodus is a highly engaging, story-driven masterpiece. What I love about the Metro trilogy is how one informs the other, and all three games somehow feel like part of the same experience even though technically they don't really follow on from one another. Unless you have grown up with the franchise, that may be hard to understand, but I love all of the Metro games equally as a single experience. You really must start with 2033 and work your way through in order to truly appreciate the evolution of the franchise. The Metro trilogy is a solid 10/10 in my book.

Imagine replacing the politically-kladeoscopic setting that was post-apocalyptic Moscow with shallow parodies akin to Mad Max and Fallout? That's Exodus for you...

The gameplay's pretty neat tho.

- insane levels of detail when it comes to immersion, characters, etc. fun gameplay
- youre actually likely to end up liking the characters
- maps are interesting for the most part and nice to explore
- graphics very nice
however
- VERY buggy and the save system is weird
- doesnt tell you when you'll need gas masks in some parts so its possible to get softlocked
One of the best, and maybe even the best game you can still play today. The vibe of these places will always be pleasing to the eye.

This is an immersive and impressive experience. this is the first time i enjoyed a FPS. the mix of open ended and linear levels are a good combination. would highly suggest this game for someone willing to put in 30-40 hours as you will be spending time sneaking and looting.
The Metro series has such a poetic portrayal of dieing from radition. With a hidden moral system entangled in its epic journey i have nothing but good things to say.

Great game and the few hours I've gotten the game to work have been fun. I just wish they'd come and update the game because of all the crashes cause it seems like its not just me and even worse on the enhanced edition.
its a good game i guess, but you need to turn on DX11 so the game doesnt randomly crash

It is such a pleasure to play this game. Feels so real. till now i find it wel thought out, not just scary for no reason. Its a great mix of everything that a FPS story mode should have.

Better to play the previous series first to deeply understand the world and characters but the game itself covers and answers most the questions the story brings within and all chapters feel unique and memorable which makes this game perfect.
